The page notes the walk is doable from London via trains to Cardiff, Abergavenny or Neath/Swansea connecting to buses. The shortcut removes 4.7 km/2.9 mi and 156m ascent, giving approximate figures of 13.4 km/8.4 mi for the shorter variant, but this is calculated rather than stated. The extension adds 4.3 km/2.7 mi but a standalone 5.7 km sub-loop is also mentioned — it is unclear whether this sub-loop is intended as a separate extractable walk variant.

clydach-gorge.title: The Clydach Gorge
clydach-gorge.notes: The walk is described in both directions (Brynmawr→Abergavenny and Abergavenny→Brynmawr) with slightly different distances and times. Brynmawr Bus Station is not a mainline railway station; travel links to train stations are via connecting bus services. The endpoint (or startpoint) Abergavenny is a mainline station, but Brynmawr Bus Station requires bus connection — requiresBus=true has been set for both directions accordingly. | Walk Options mention bus lines A3 and 78 enabling shorter versions of the route, but no specific shorter walk distances or endpoints are clearly defined as distinct named variants beyond ad-hoc cuts using bus stops along the A465.

walks.ratingExplanation: Perhaps an idiosyncratic love of mine, as this isn't a popular hiking spot, but I find it exceptionally charming. This route takes you through what little remains of the once-thriving Roman city of Calleva Atrebatum, including the original walls, and a large amphitheatre where gladiatorial combats entertained up to 7,000 people at a time

walks.miscellany: Begins, necessarily, with a lengthy suburban segment, but then becomes very beautiful and surprisingly quiet. Particularly lovely on the walk along the cliffs east of Folkestone, where there are also a number of lunch spot choices. The narrow overgrown paths are best. But note that there is a LOT of steep ascents and descents on this walk.
